subject: Sports
title: Carb Loading: Race Day Fuel
description: One-minute skill: Carb Loading: Race Day Fuel.

hook.text: The night before a marathon, elite runners eat mountains of pasta. There's real science behind it.

buildup.text: Muscles store energy as glycogen. A full glycogen store provides about 90 minutes of intense running fuel.

discovery.text: Carb loading—eating extra carbohydrates for 2-3 days before a race—can increase glycogen stores by up to 50%.

twist.text: Early methods involved a 'depletion phase' of no carbs, then a binge. Modern methods skip the suffering.

climax.text: Combined with mid-race gels and drinks, carb loading helps marathoners maintain pace through the dreaded 'wall.'

punchline.text: Eat pasta, store glycogen, run farther. Fuel science made simple.

quiz.question: What does carb loading do for endurance athletes?
options: Increases glycogen stores in muscles by up to 50%
Reduces body weight for faster running
Builds new muscle before the race
